Reporting to the Senior Marketing Operations Specialist, the intern will gain hands\-on experience across multiple areas of marketing operations, with a primary focus on **content management and governance**, while also supporting **sales enablement, internal communications, and marketing analytics**.


The role offers strong exposure to EMEA and Global teams, contributing to the efficient execution and continuous improvement of marketing processes and tools.

* Support the maintenance and governance of content platforms, including content quality checks, lifecycle tracking, and alignment with regulatory requirements (e.g., EU MDR, product updates, patient\-facing materials).
* Assist in organizing and publishing marketing materials across downstream channels, including the Sales Enablement platform, ensuring content availability, consistency, and correct linkage across systems.
* Contribute to content optimization activities, such as platform clean\-ups, structure alignment, and basic monitoring of content usage and performance trends.
* Support internal communication activities through Microsoft Sharepoint, including the preparation and publication of the weekly divisional newsletter and process\-related updates.

* Provide support to marketing dashboards and reporting activities (Salesforce \& Tableau), including data validation, updates, and preparation of insights for internal reviews.
* Collaborate with EMEA and Global teams, participating in alignment meetings and supporting ongoing projects and process improvement

 **What Are We Looking For in You:**

* Working in an international environment;
* Attractive benefits package;
* Inspirational colleagues \& culture;
* Possibility to make an impact;

Our portfolio of devices and therapies helps physicians diagnose and treat complex cardiovascular, respiratory, digestive, oncological, neurological and urological diseases and conditions. title: Senior Representative - Outside Sales
description:
As a Senior Representative \- Outside Sales, you will be responsible for developing new prospects and interacting with assigned customers to increase sales and margin of the Company's products and/or services. You will participate in customer business planning to understand customer needs. Your primary focus will be face\-to\-face selling and account management activities to meet sales revenue goals by assessing customer needs and suggesting appropriate products, services and/or solutions and ensure a smooth sales process. You will engage with all levels of the customer’s organization including technical, operations, supply chain and executive management and may develop and deliver sales bids, presentations, proposals and/or product demonstrations.  



**Responsibilities:**

* Qualify accounts by determining market potential and provides periodic territory sales forecasts.
* Execute and expand assigned customer account plan(s) which is developed in conjunction with management.
* Execute on billing margin initiatives through value added services, marketing programs, and supplier engagement.
* Prospect potential customers, including cold calling and developing leads through referral channels.
* Communicate with customers and leads to identify and understand their product or service needs; identifies and suggests products and services to meet those needs.
* Demonstrate the functions and utility of products or services to customers based on their needs.
* Ensure customer satisfaction through ongoing communication and relationship management; resolves any issues that may arise post\-sale.
* Maintain communication with existing and previous customers, alerting them of new products, services, and enhancements that may be of interest.
* Develop sales/supplier plans to drive account growth and retention while utilizing sales management tools to track and measure progress.
* Develop and grows product knowledge through Wesco and supplier training.
* Develop strong relationships with suppliers, including performing regular joint sales calls.
* Provide quotations directly or in conjunction with sales support team.
Mentor sales team and communicates relevant information and expectations for optimum customer service.

* Ability to work flexible schedule and occasional overnight travel
* Excellent sales and negotiation skills
* Ability to develop and deliver presentations

title: Finance Manager – Southern Europe
description:
**Position Title:** Finance Manager \- Hybrid


**Location:** Vimercate, Italy


**Hybrid :** 1 day/week in local office, remainder remote.


**Employment:** Permanent


**Come build your career.**

It takes great people to achieve greatness. People with a sense of purpose and integrity. People with a relentless pursuit of excellence. People who care about making things better For Those Who Make The World™. Sound like you? Join our top\-notch team of approximately 48,500 diverse and high\-performing professionals globally who are making their mark on some of the world’s most beloved brands, including DEWALT®, BLACK\+DECKER®, CRAFTSMAN®, STANLEY®, CUB CADET®, and HUSTLER®.


**The Job:**

As the **Finance Manager** , you’ll be part of our **Southern Europe Finance** team. You’ll get to:


* Lead the month\-end close process to ensure timely and accurate financial results, including variance analysis and supporting commentary.
* Lead monthly forecasting, quarterly reviews, strategic planning, and budgeting processes for Southern Europe across all P\&L elements through operating margin, both at total company and brand level.
* Own the SG\&A spend and forecast process across all categories and functions, with particular focus on Compensation \& Benefits, Marketing, and Investment spend.
* Develop customer P\&L statements for key customers and partner with local finance and sales teams to drive profitable regional growth.
* Provide senior management and commercial teams with in\-depth analysis of historical and forecasted trends across key performance indicators, supporting projections and scenario planning.
* Recommend financial actions by evaluating alternatives aligned with business objectives and strategic priorities.
* Lead the Sales and Operational Planning (S\&OP) process for the region.
* Prepare and deliver clear, impactful presentations and financial updates to senior management.
* Collaborate with senior management, commercial teams, and finance stakeholders to track strategic initiatives and support cross\-functional business projects.
* Drive process improvement and automation initiatives in liaison with the Center of Excellence to enhance efficiency, control, and decision support.

**The Person:**

You love to learn and grow and be acknowledged for your valuable contributions. You’re not intimidated by innovation. In fact, you embrace it. Wouldn’t it be great if you could do your job and do a world of good? You also have:


* Degree in Accounting, Finance, or MBA equivalent; additional professional certifications are an advantage.
* Strong experience in financial analysis, forecasting, budgeting, and business planning within a dynamic multinational environment, preferably FMCG.
* Advanced Excel skills and experience with enterprise systems and analytical tools, including SAP, EPM, and Power BI.
* Excellent analytical, communication, and presentation skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders and support senior decision\-making.
* Proficiency in Italian and English is required; Spanish and Greek are considered a plus.

**The Details:**

You’ll receive a competitive salary and a great benefits plan, including:


* Competitive compensation package.
* Comprehensive benefits in line with local market practice and company policy.
* Hybrid working model with 1 day/week in the local office and the remainder remote.
* Career development opportunities in a large international organisation.
* Discounts on Stanley Black \& Decker tools and other partner programs.

**And More:**

We want our company to be a place you’ll want to be – and stay. Being part of our team means you’ll get to:


**Grow:** Be part of our global company with 20\+ brands to grow and develop your skills along multiple career paths.


**Learn:** Have access to a wealth of learning resources, including our digital learning portal.


**Belong:** Experience an awesome place to work, where we have mutual respect and a great appreciation for a wide range of perspectives and experiences.


**Give Back:** Help us continue to make positive changes locally and globally through volunteerism, giving back, and sustainable business practices.


What’s more, you’ll get that pride that comes from empowering makers, doers, protectors, and everyday heroes all over the world. We’re more than the \#1 tools company. More than a driving force in outdoor power equipment. More than a global leader in industrial. We’re visionaries and innovators. As successful as we’ve been in the past, we have so much further to go. That’s where you come in. Join us!


*Salary Range: €55,000\.00 \- €70,000\.00*

*This is the lowest to highest salary range we would pay at the time of this posting. The salary offered in the range may vary depending on multiple factors including job level, geographic location, job related knowledge, skills, qualifications and experience.*

*The salary range listed in this job posting reflects the range for the primary location specified. The salary range may vary for other locations.*

*Please note that salary is only one component of total rewards at Stanley Black \& Decker.*

*The successful candidate may be eligible for annual merit increases.*

*The salary range and associated pay elements are established in line with Stanley Black \& Decker’s compensation structure. If collective bargaining agreements, sectoral agreements, works council arrangements, or statutory pay frameworks apply, they determine the salary range and additional pay elements.*

***We Don’t Just Build The World, We Build Innovative Technology Too.***

Joining the Stanley Black \& Decker team means working in an innovative, tech\-driven and highly collaborative team environment supported by over 43,500 professionals in 60 countries across the globe. Here, you’ll get the unique chance to impact some of the world’s most iconic brands including STANLEY TOOLS, DEWALT, CRAFTSMAN, MAC TOOLS and Black \+ Decker. Your ideas and solutions have the potential to reach millions of customers as we work together to write the next chapter in our history. Come build with us and take your career to new heights.


**Who We Are**

We’re the World’s largest tool company. We’re industry visionaries. We’re solving problems and advancing the manufacturing trade through innovative technology and our Industry 4\.0 Initiative. We are committed to ensuring our state\-of\-the\-art “smart factory” products and services provide greater quality to our customers \& greater environmental and social value to our planet. We are unique in that we have a rich and storied history dating back to 1843, but that hasn't stopped us from evolving into a vibrant, diverse, global growth company.


**Benefits \& Perks**

You’ll get a competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits plan that includes medical, dental, life, vision, wellness program, disability, retirement benefits, Employee Stock Purchase Plan, Paid Time Off, including paid vacation, holidays \& personal days, and tuition reimbursement. And, of course, discounts on Stanley Black \& Decker tools and products and well as discount programs for many other vendors and partners.


**What You’ll Also Get**

Career Opportunity: Career paths aren’t linear here. Being part of our global company with 60\+ brands gives you the chance to grow and develop your skills along multiple career paths.


**Learning \& Development:**  

Our lifelong learning philosophy means you’ll have access to a wealth of state\-of\-the\-art learning resources, including our Lean Academy and online university (where you can get certificates and specializations from renowned colleges and universities).


**Diverse \& Inclusive Culture:**  

We pride ourselves on being an awesome place to work. We respect and embrace differences because that’s how the best work gets done. You’ll find we like to have fun here, too.


**Purpose\-Driven Company:**  

You’ll help us continue to make positive changes in the local communities where we work and live as well as in the broader world through volunteerism, giving back and sustainable business practices.


**EEO Statement:**  

All qualified applicants to Stanley Black \& Decker are considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran’s status or any other protected characteristic.


If you require reasonable accommodation to complete an application or access our website, please contact us at (860\) 827\-3923 or at accommodations@sbdinc.com . Due to volume, we cannot respond to unrelated inquiries about the status of a completed application or resetting an account password.

title: KERING Production Project Manager
description:
**Summary**

About Kering
  

Kering is a global, family\-led luxury group, home to people whose passion and expertise nurture creative Houses across couture and ready\-to\-wear, leather goods, jewelry, eyewear and beauty: Gucci, Saint Laurent, Bottega Veneta, Balenciaga, McQueen, Brioni, Boucheron, Pomellato, Dodo, Qeelin, Ginori 1735, as well as Kering Eyewear and Kering Beauté. Inspired by their creative heritage, Kering Houses design and craft exceptional products and experiences that reflect the Group’s commitment to excellence, sustainability and culture. This vision is expressed in our signature: Creativity is our Legacy. In 2025, Kering employed 44,000 people and generated revenue of €14\.7 billion.
**Job Description**

We are currently seeking an Production Project Manager to join our dynamic team based in Milan, reporting to the Head of Tech Production \& Aftersales. The team is mainly split between Milan and Florence, working with global stakeholders in Kering and its Maisons.


***Your opportunity***

You will play a key role within the Technologies Production \& Aftersales Area, working on processes, area’s applications and systems supporting the luxury processes and their various interfaces, in strict collaboration with the Kering Brands and all the other involved Technologies people. You will manage program and projects’ roadmap, define objectives with stakeholders; ensure deployment on time/on budget, decisions are taken, risks are identified/mitigated, issues are solved. Projects will span from Production to Aftersales, from Traceability \& RFID to Sustainability in Supply Chain. Working in an international environment, you will keep one step ahead of the technology curve, identifying change needs and share best practice to streamline business processes.


***How you will contribute***

Define clear direction for projects by defining project scope and objectives, creating the project plan (timeline, milestones, dependencies), estimating resources (people, tools, budget) and identifying initial risks and constraints


Be a point of contact for the projects and the assigned activities, identifying and managing dependencies with other projects and streams, engaging stakeholders on goals and status, facilitating collaboration between technical teams, vendors, business leads, managing expectations and resolving conflicts, ensuring proper understanding of tasks and priorities for assigned people


Ensure proper execution of initiatives, by assigning tasks to technical team members, monitoring progress (stand\-ups, sprint reviews, or status meetings), tracking deliverables and removing blockers and ensuring quality standards are met; also collaborate in the functional and technical analysis on the basis of the solution to be achieved. Finally ensure transition to run mode


Work proactively on Risk Management and Problem Resolution, by identifying risks early, implementing mitigation or contingency plans, resolving technical and organizational issues quickly, making informed decisions to keep the project stable


Ensure project’s transparency through Reporting \& Project Performance Tracking, by monitoring KPIs (timeline, budget, scope, quality), preparing progress reports for management, providing forecasts (cost, time, resources) and ensuring documentation is complete


***Who you are***

A professional with proven experience delivering large\-scale, complex, and international IT Supply Chain programs and good knowledge on Supply Chain/Manufacturing processes (in Luxury/Retail Industry is a plus)


7–10 years of experience in IT Project Management


A highly organized person, a critical\-thinker, able to set priorities and work effectively with tight deadlines


Proactive, team worker, diplomatic and able to handle good relationships both with internal and external stakeholders


You have excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to communicate effectively with stakeholders from different functions and locations


Fluent in English, both written and spoken; Italian and French as a plus


***What we offer:***

* CCNL Applied: National Collective Labour Agreement per gli Addetti alle Industrie Manifatturiere delle Pelli e Succedanei e Ombrelli e Ombrelloni
* Indicative starting compensation: 40 000€
* Annual bonus opportunity according to company internal agreement
* Benefits: in addition to those provided under the applicable National Collective Labor Agreement and by law, further benefits and initiatives may be offered in accordance with company policies (e.g. company canteen, ..)

*Kering is committed to building a diverse workforce. We believe diversity in all its forms – gender, age, nationality, culture, religious beliefs and sexual orientation – enriches the workplace. It opens up opportunities for people to express their talent, both individually and collectively and it helps foster our ability to adapt to a changing world. As an Equal Opportunity Employer we welcome and consider applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of their background* *.*

Regular
**Start Date**

2026\-09\-01
**Schedule**

Full time

**Artificial Intelligence. Actual Impact.**
At Docebo, we’re using AI to change how people learn at work—and we mean actually change it. We’re an AI\-powered learning platform that helps organizations create, deliver, and manage training all in one place. But our real mission goes deeper: we help teams move faster, work smarter, and focus on the work that truly matters. Our platform is built with intelligent, time\-saving tools that personalize learning, eliminate busywork, and turn training from a checkbox into a superpower. The result? Better experiences for learners and real results for businesses.

  

We’re shaping the future of learning with a team that isn’t afraid to challenge the status quo. If you're excited by the idea of using AI to make work\-life better for real people–you’ll feel right at home here. And it’s not just what we build, it’s how we show up. At Docebo, our values aren’t just posters on the wall—they guide how we work every day. We call it the **Docebo Heart**: trust by default, assume positive intent, and create space for different perspectives to thrive.

  

So… what are you waiting for? Join 900\+ Docebians around the world and help us reinvent the way people learn, because learning never stops.

 **Summary:**


As the Senior Product Engineer II you play a pivotal role in guiding the software development and product engineering teams through highly complex technical challenges, requiring deep analytical and problem\-solving skills. You will start with a unique blend of hands\-on, high\-impact development and technical leadership, evolving into a strategic orchestrator as your team grows to eliminate planning complexities and streamline cross\-team communication. This isn't about just maintaining code; it’s about driving systemic efficiency, setting elite engineering standards, and transforming technical strategy into reality to fuel Docebo's growth.

  


The Day\-to\-Day ️

* **Crush Complexity:** Lead the analysis and resolution of complex technical issues by applying advanced knowledge and evaluating a variety of factors to determine the most effective methods and procedures.
* **Architect Innovation:** Develop and implement innovative solutions to address intricate technical challenges, utilizing in\-depth professional expertise and understanding of company objectives.
* **Elevate Talent:** Mentor and guide team members in applying advanced concepts and techniques, fostering professional growth and ensuring the team’s work aligns with departmental goals.
* **Bridge the Disciplines:** Collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams, including Product Management and Testing, to ensure the successful integration of different disciplines and the effective execution of projects.
* **Advise and Lead:** Act as a technical advisor on complex projects, occasionally stepping in for the Engineering Manager, to ensure that technical direction is consistent with company standards and objectives.
* **Drive Continuous Improvement:** Evaluate and adapt complex models and procedures, guiding the development of new policies and ideas that enhance technical operations.
* **Own the Outcomes:** Ensure that technical work is consistent with departmental objectives by applying judgment in selecting methods and evaluating outcomes.


Your Superpowers

* **Master the Stack:** Exhibit advanced, wide\-ranging experience and in\-depth professional knowledge across both backend and frontend development.
* **Crush Complexity:** Prove adept at resolving complex issues creatively and effectively, understanding the interrelationships of different disciplines.
* **Lead and Mentor:** Demonstrate strong leadership abilities, focusing on mentoring team members and ensuring alignment with company objectives.
* **Bring the Experience:** Leverage 7\+ years of experience in software development, with significant experience in both backend and frontend technologies.
* **Technical Versatility:** You bring proficiency in **NodeJS** and **Angular.** Any experience with other languages like PHP, Python, or Golang is also valued but you are willing to work with **NodeJS**.
* **Drive Innovation:** Apply a proven track record in leading and mentoring technical teams, with a demonstrated ability to develop and implement innovative technical solutions.
* **Navigate the Process:** Utilize a strong understanding of complex software development processes and the ability to apply and adapt advanced techniques to achieve departmental objectives.
* **Decide with Precision:** Practice excellent judgment and decision\-making skills in selecting and evaluating methods and procedures for achieving results.
* **Shape the Future:** Direct the application of existing principles and guide the development of new policies and ideas in a collaborative and cross\-functional environment.

**Our Hybrid Work Philosophy** 


Great work can happen anywhere but coming together helps us go further. Our team spends three days a week in the office (Tuesday\-Thursday) to collaborate, solve problems, and learn from each other. With flexibility the rest of the week, it’s a balance designed to help everyone do their best work and keep growing.

**Our Total Rewards Philosophy** 


Our Total Rewards Philosophy centers around three core areas to reward and care for our People:

* **Rewarding Impact:** We lead with competitive pay to reward the impact, skills and traits that fuel our success.
* **Fostering Holistic Wellbeing:** We care deeply about and invest in the whole person with programs that support our people’s physical, mental, and financial well\-being.
* **Empowering Our Talent Culture:** We build a culture of trust and empowerment by designing our rewards and benefits with transparency, equity, and flexibility, enabling our people to do their best work and stay for the long haul.

 **Our Promise to You**  

* **Financial Wellness:** Own a piece of Docebo through our Employee Share Purchase Plan (ESPP) at a 15% discount, plus a competitive compensation package.
* **Your Well\-Being, Covered:** You’ll get access to health benefits, so you can get the care you need when you need it.
* **Rest, Relax, Repeat:** Rest and recharge with paid vacation days, two company\-wide Docebo Days, floating holidays for cultural celebrations, and your birthday off!
* **Family First**: We provide coverage offering you time with your little one(s) so you can soak up all those precious moments. Fun fact: we had 30 Docebian babies join the family in 2025!
* **Connections That Count:** Connect with global communities through our Employee Resource Groups (including PRIDE, DWA, BIDOC, and Green Ambassadors) and company\-wide events that keep the fun rolling all year long.

**About Docebo** 


At Docebo, we create seamless, AI\-powered learning experiences for over 3,000 customers worldwide. We have successfully achieved two IPOs (TSX: DCBO \& NASDAQ: DCBO), been recognized as a top SaaS e\-learning solution, and are growing exponentially in the process. We're a global company, with office across North America, EMEA, APAC, and beyond. Our team is guided by six core values—**Innovation, Simplicity, Accountability, Togetherness, Curiosity, and Impact**—that shape everything we do. If this resonates with you, now is the perfect time to join one of the fastest\-growing learning technology companies in the world.


Docebo is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We are committed to diversity and inclusion in our workforce. All qualified applicants and employees will receive consideration for employment regardless of their race, colour, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity, and sexual orientation), national origin, citizenship status, age, disability, genetic information, or any other category protected under applicable law.


As a federal contractor, Docebo is committed to the principles of affirmative action and equal employment opportunity for protected veterans and individuals with disabilities. Docebo does not discriminate because of protected veteran status or on the basis of disability, and Docebo takes affirmative action to employ and advance in employment qualified protected veterans and individuals with disabilities.


Any individuals requiring a reasonable accommodation or would like to voluntarily disclose a disability or protected veteran status to assist with their employment application should send an e\-mail to recruiting\_accommodations@docebo.com. The email should also include the position you’re interested in.

title: Dealflow Associate
description:
**About Ancora**

**Ancora** is building AI\-native accounting software that replaces traditional management systems. We combine a technology platform that automates operational accounting work with a roll\-up strategy: acquiring and consolidating accounting firms across Italy.


We're venture\-backed, Milan\-based, with first studio acquisitions underway. The product is greenfield, zero legacy code, modern stack, built from scratch. We own the problem end\-to\-end: we build the technology, acquire the firms, and operate the service.

 **About the Role**


Ancora's thesis depends on a simple, compounding advantage: we need to acquire and integrate more accounting studios, faster and better, than anyone else in Italy. Most of our targets are senior professionisti, often in their 50s and 60s, who have built their firm over decades and tend to be sceptical about anything labelled "tech" or "consolidation".

  

As **M\&A Associate (aka Dealflow Associate)**, you are the person on the other side of that conversation. You proactively identify the right studios, you start warming them up, you explain who Ancora is, what we are building, and how a deal with us looks. The relationship and the trust come first; the term sheet comes after.

  

This is roughly 70% of the role: identifying targets, opening conversations, qualifying, and bringing senior counterparties along to a real first meeting with the founders. The remaining 30% is execution support on live deals, where you help with due diligence, financial analysis, and the logistics of getting a deal to closing.

  

The non\-negotiable trait is **people maturity**. You spend most of your time on the phone with people twice your age, who have run their own business for decades. You need to be kind, respectful, genuinely curious, while also being firm and not letting yourself be brushed off or talked down to. Most candidates can do one or the other. The best ones do both.


What You'll Do

* **Build the target map.** Identify and prioritize Italian accounting studios using public data (Camera di Commercio, Albo, LinkedIn, Google Maps), third\-party sources and referrals. Density\-first geography, ownership intent, succession dynamics.
* **Open conversations and warm targets up.** Lead the multi\-channel outreach (email, LinkedIn, phone, in\-person follow\-up where it matters). The first conversation is rarely about a transaction; it is about credibility. You earn the second meeting.
* **Tell the Ancora story well.** Explain who we are, what we are building, why we are different, and how a deal with us looks. Adapt the message to the partner you have in front of you. Stay accurate, never overpromise.
* **Qualify carefully.** Run first screening conversations with studio owners. Assess size, client concentration, P\&L indicators, ownership intent, succession dynamics, and cultural fit. Filter signal from noise.
* **Manage the CRM and pipeline.** Keep the pipeline clean, updated, and decision\-useful. Drive weekly and bi\-weekly reviews with the COO and founders. No conversation falls through the cracks.
* **Support deal execution.** On qualified targets, help run due diligence: normalize P\&Ls, analyze revenue quality and client concentration, build valuation references, support LOI drafting and closing logistics.
* **Feed the operating model.** Surface patterns, objections, pricing dynamics, and market intelligence to the founding team. Every conversation should make Ancora a little smarter about the market.

 **What We're Looking For:**

 **Must Have**

* **2–4 years of experience** in roles where you have proactively built and managed relationships with senior counterparties: investor relations, M\&A boutique, investment banking, private equity, business development, strategic partnerships, search fund, fundraising, advisory, or B2B sales toward decision makers.
* **People maturity.** You can hold your ground in a conversation with a 60\-year\-old professionista and still leave them feeling respected. Warm without being soft, firm without being abrasive.
* **Strong written and phone presence.** You build trust in five minutes. People who do not usually pick up the phone, pick up for you.
* **Execution discipline.** Clean CRM, relentless follow\-ups, process orientation. You do not lose leads, you do not drop conversations, you close loops.
* **Financial literacy.** You can read a P\&L, understand normalization, and engage in a basic valuation discussion. You do not need to be the modeller, but you need to understand what the numbers mean.
* **Italian native or near\-native**, with professional English.

 **Nice to Have**

* Background in M\&A boutique, investment banking, private equity, transaction services, search fund, or roll\-up platform.
* Investor Relations or fundraising experience at a scale\-up, asset manager, PE firm or venture fund.
* Direct exposure to accounting, tax, or professional services firms.
* Experience with outbound tooling (Apollo, Instantly, Lemlist, Clay, HubSpot, Salesforce). Useful, not core.
* Familiarity with the Italian SME landscape and the world of commercialisti.

 **Mindset**

* **Trusted by default.** People want to talk to you. Senior counterparties take you seriously.
* **Kind but firm.** Respectful without being submissive. You do not take a soft "ci pensiamo" as a no, and you do not take a hard one personally.
* **Proactive.** You do not wait for inbound. You build the funnel and you keep it full.
* **Operator, not pure analyst.** You care about deals advancing, not about the most elegant model.
* **Executional machine.** What you say you will do, you do. Always. People who work with you stop checking up on you within a month.

 **Why This Role is Interesting**

* **Direct leverage on a thesis.** The number of studios Ancora closes this year depends in large part on what you do every day.
* **A real roll\-up from day one.** You are not running a spreadsheet on theoretical targets. You own a live pipeline with real conversations.
* **Craft of relationship\-driven origination.** You build a personal network of senior professionisti and a reputation in a market that values both. This is a skill you keep for the rest of your career.
* **Exposure to everything.** Sourcing, relationship\-building, qualification, DD, valuation, negotiation, closing. In most firms these are five different jobs; here it is one role.
* **Founder proximity.** You work directly with the founding team and the COO. Your judgment shapes acquisition decisions.
* **Category\-defining market.** Italian accounting is fragmented, mandatory, recurring, and has never been consolidated properly. You build the map.

 **What We Offer**

* **Office First.** Collaboration is easier and more effective in person in our Milan HQ. You can work from home up to 30% of the time, with three core days in the office.
* **Compensation.** €50,000 gross annual salary \+ performance bonus tied to pipeline and deal milestones (we expect the bonus for this position to be 20\-30k in 2026, 30\-40k in 2027\)
* **Equity.** Meaningful stock options package, with 0 strike price and strong upside potential given the stage of the company.
* **Benefits.** Meal vouchers (10 euro x day) and fringe benefits (1k x year).
* **Team.** Reports directly to COO. Works closely with the founding team on every active deal.
* **Autonomy.** Strong voice on sourcing strategy, target prioritization, and outreach playbook.
* **Growth.** As the platform scales, clear path toward deeper deal ownership and, over time, broader corporate development scope.

 **How to Apply**


Send your CV to **talent@ancora.work** with a brief note on why this role interests you. Tell us about one real relationship you built with a senior counterparty (in M\&A, IR, BD, sales, or otherwise), what made it work, and what you actually got from it.

 *We're an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and encourage applications from people of all backgrounds.*

Job description
  
  

**Job Description:**  

Join our team and start a new adventure in an international and dynamic environment where you will be able to fulfil your career expectations in a fast\-growing organization. **What We Offer:**  

An international community bringing together 110\+ different nationalities
  
**An environment where trust has a central place:** 70% of our key leaders started their careers at the first level of responsibility
  
A robust training system with our internal Academy and 250\+ available modules
  
A vibrant workplace that frequently gathers for internal events (afterworks, team buildings, etc.)
  
At Mantu, sustainability is part of everything we do. You’ll have the opportunity to turn your ideas into action and make a tangible impact. Every day, our teams bring our ESG commitments to life, from reducing our footprint to driving positive change within our communities. Through our WeCare Together program, you’ll be empowered to design and lead projects that create real social or environmental impact, with the company’s full support\[doc2].
  
  

Amaris Consulting is proud to be an equal\-opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics.
  
  

Who are we?
  
Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects for over a decade – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. Our solutions focus on four different Business Lines: Information System \& Digital, Telecom, Life Sciences and Engineering. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential. Amaris is your steppingstone to cross rivers of change, meet challenges and achieve all your projects with success.
  
  

At Amaris, we strive to provide our candidates with the best possible recruitment experience. We like to get to know our candidates, challenge them, and be able to give them proper feedback as quickly as possible. Here's what our recruitment process looks like:
  
  

**Brief Call:** Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you!
  
  

Interviews (the average number of interviews is 3 \- the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your line manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. We will talk in depth about you, your experience, and skills, but also about the position and what will be expected of you. Of course, you will also get to know Amaris: our culture, our roots, our teams, and your career opportunities!
  
  

**Case study:** Depending on the position, we may ask you to take a test. This could be a role play, a technical assessment, a problem\-solving scenario, etc.
  
  

As you know, every person is different and so is every role in a company. That is why we have to adapt accordingly, and the process may differ slightly at times. However, please know that we always put ourselves in the candidate's shoes to ensure they have the best possible experience.
  
We look forward to meeting you!

title: Internship - Jr. Quality Engineer (m/f/d)
description:
**Company Description**  

The **Bosch Group** is a leading global supplier of technology and services. It employs roughly 410,000 associates worldwide. The company generated sales of 78\.5 billion euros in 2018\. Its operations are divided into four business sectors: Mobility Solutions, Industrial Technology, Consumer Goods, and Energy and Building Technology. As a leading IoT company, Bosch offers innovative solutions for smart homes, smart cities, connected mobility, and connected manufacturing. It uses its expertise in sensor technology, software, and services, as well as its own IoT cloud, to offer its customers connected, crossdomain solutions from a single source. The Bosch Group’s strategic objective is to create solutions for a connected life.


The special ownership structure of Robert Bosch GmbH guarantees the entrepreneurial freedom of the Bosch Group, making it possible for the company to plan over the long term and to undertake significant upfront investments in the safeguarding of its future.


Welcome to a place where your ideas lead to something big. Welcome to Bosch.


As a global partner, **Bosch Rexroth** supports mechanical and plant engineering challenges around the world with its cutting\-edge technology and unique industry knowledge. More than 32,300 employees worldwide work on safe, efficient, intelligent and powerful solutions. For example, they help in the economical production of small batch sizes, or save energy while simultaneously raising productivity. With our cross\-technology portfolio, digital services and comprehensive service, we are your partner for machines and plants.


Today you can be part of this world. We are looking for great talents and the next one could be YOU.


Apply!


WE MOVE. YOU WIN.

 **Job Description** **What will you take care of?**


As a Junior Quality Engineer you will be part of the **Quality Management \& Methods office**, in our Head Quarter in Cernusco sul Naviglio (MI). You will give a cross support impacting all company functions and processes in order to implement a continuous improvement.


You Will:

***Help shaping the future****:* supporting the Quality Management team in daily activities, operational support for quality management system, quality assurance and auditing.

***Take on responsibility****:* taking care of quality assurance, analyzing, recording and following\-up quality deviations in order to contribute to the continuous improvement.

***Experience cooperation****:* supporting the technical departments for process improvements and lessons learned. You will be involved in auditing activities both internal and at our sales locations.

***Experience internationality****:* experiencing relationship with the other Countries within Bosch Rexroth South Europe Region (France, Spain, Greece) and with our Head Quarter in Germany.

 **Qualifications** **What distinguishes you?**

***Education:*** You are a recent graduate in Industrial Engineering/ Management Engineering.

***Know\-how/Organizational Skills:*** You are a result\-oriented person and you have the ability to adapt to different contexts, as well as an aptitude for problem solving. You are also very organized and able to deal with strict deadlines. You are familiar with management tools.

***Personal Skills:*** You have an analytical mindset and relational attitude as well, being precise, reliable and team\-worker.

***Languages:*** Fluent Italian and English

 **Curricular or Extracurricular internship**

 **Additional Information**  

Bosch is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin or ancestry, age, disability or any other protected class

title: Underwriter Assistant, Italy
description:
**“Underwriting assistant”**

You will contribute to the growth and profitability of AIG AESA unit by providing administration support to specific Lines of Business in Italy, assisting the UW community. You will serve both internal \& external customers by completing administrative tasks for specific LOB underwriters; responding to customer requests; resolving complaints \& maintaining data quality in systems. Consistently meet service levels and performance targets in terms of volume, accuracy and quality.  

This role reports to the Country Operations Manager.

 **The responsibilities for this role include:**  

* Partner with underwriters and COE partners for end\-to\-end processing of new,renewal business as well as endorsements.
* Participate in pre\-binding activities.
* Liaising with brokers/clients, gather information necessary to prepare quotes(either for new submissions or renewals) and update raters, update raters whendeemed necessary
* Prepare the referral documentation
* Issue quotes upon receiving instructions from the Underwiter
* Decline submission request if not within UW guidelines

Assist w. data/portfolio management  

Assist with Data Integrity together with underwriting: Rate Change, actual totechnical, linesize, limits and premium reports are checked against rater andunderwriters view to ensure consistency.

That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family.

**Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world**

title: IT Consultant Cyber Security - Milan | BCG Platinion
description:
Who We Are


Do you want to turn the challenges of digitalization into the solutions of the future? Join BCG Platinion in tackling the core IT issues of leading global companies. Deploy customized platforms, architectures, strategies, and organizations to leverage the digital potential of our customers and drive true innovation, with state\-of\-the\-art tools such as cloud computing, AI, blockchain, microservices, and containerization. Add your individual strengths to our unique team, always there to support you. Together with Boston Consulting Group and our clients, we turn digital visions into reality.

  

What You'll Do

* Cybersecurity Leadership and Strategy: Lead the development and implementation of comprehensive cybersecurity roadmaps and strategic initiatives, aligning them with client objectives and digital transformation goals. Drive end\-to\-end security solutions from conceptual design to implementation, ensuring robust security architectures and frameworks are in place.
* Client Engagement and Project Management: Manage and oversee complex cybersecurity projects, ensuring excellence in delivery while maintaining stringent timelines and budgets. Provide expert security advisory and support to clients throughout major IT transformation programs, helping them achieve and maintain key security certifications such as ISO27001\.
* Innovative Solution Development: Utilize advanced technologies like AI, blockchain, and cloud computing to design and refine innovative cybersecurity solutions. Tailor strategies to include aspects of identity \& access governance, data protection, security monitoring, and network protection.
* Team Leadership and Development: Cultivate a supportive and inclusive team environment, promoting continuous learning and professional growth. Mentor team members, oversee the recruitment of top\-tier talent, and foster a culture of excellence and innovation.
* Vendor and Stakeholder Management: Effectively select and manage relationships with security vendors, services, and solution providers to enhance the security offerings provided to clients.

  

What You'll Bring

* A university degree in cyber security, information technology, or a related field.
* From 4 to 6 years of relevant experience in IT security consulting or a similar role, demonstrating a track record of leading sizable and complex projects.
* Strong practical knowledge of cybersecurity operating processes and frameworks such as NIST, ISO/IEC 27000, and SABSA.
* Holders of professional certifications like CISSP, CISM, or ISO27001 are preferred.
* Exceptional leadership skills with the ability to manage interdisciplinary teams and complex stakeholder relationships.
* Excellent communication skills in both Italian and English are required
* Comfortable working in global contexts and adaptable to rapidly changing environments.
* Readiness to travel extensively as required by client engagements and project demands.

  

Additional info


At BCG Platinion, our people and relationships are at the heart of everything we do. We believe that in\-person collaboration plays an essential role in our culture, mentorship, and professional development. That's why we generally operate on a hybrid model, with office presence shaped by business needs, team norms, and local policies and practices. We aim to provide a dynamic and collaborative environment that fosters connection and teamwork.

 **Compensation Information**


Total compensation for this role includes base salary, annual discretionary performance bonus, and a market\-leading benefits package described below. Our Reward Philosophy is to take a holistic approach to offer a comprehensive package which includes market\-competitive base salary, discretionary performance bonus and strong benefits that are locally relevant and support both physical and mental health. Our philosophy drives our actions and aims at being competitive, fair and easy for employees to understand. We are committed to ensuring that BCG Platinion is the best place for you to thrive and grow.


The base salary range for this role is **EUR 46\.000,00 \- EUR 49\.000,00**.


This is an estimated range. Any final offer would be based on objective, role\-related criteria such as your relevant experience, qualifications, and demonstrated skills. Employees may also be eligible for an annual discretionary performance bonus.

  

BCG Platinion offers a best\-in\-class benefits package designed to provide comprehensive care and meaningful support to you and your family. Benefits may vary based on role, contract type, and eligibility criteria.


Our benefits include:

* Health insurance: comprehensive medical coverage for employees with the option to extend coverage to eligible family members
* Meal vouchers and corporate discounts: daily meal allowance and access to a range of partner discounts
* Life/Accident /Disability Insurance for employees
* Employee Assistance Program (EAP): confidential psychological, legal, and financial support services
* Well\-being initiatives: programs supporting physical health, mental well\-being, and family needs
* Flexible benefits/Welfare: an annual flexible benefits allowance that can be used for eligible services such as education, health, transport, and leisure activities
* Company car plan: available for eligible roles

  

Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.

title: Total Reward Trainee
description:
TOTAL REWARDS TRAINEE



CHI SIAMO



Da oltre 50 anni HEINEKEN è leader del settore birrario in Italia, con una quota di mercato del 33,3% e oltre 2\.000 collaboratori su tutto il territorio nazionale. Produciamo più di 6 milioni di ettolitri di birra ogni anno nei nostri quattro birrifici situati a Comun Nuovo (BG), Pollein (AO), Massafra (TA) e Assemini (CA), a cui si aggiunge il contributo fondamentale di Partesa, la nostra rete di distribuzione e servizi dedicata al canale Ho.Re.Ca.


Grazie a una solida identità locale e una presenza globale, HEINEKEN unisce innovazione e tradizione, producendo un impatto positivo sulle comunità in cui opera.



In HEINEKEN promuoviamo un ambiente di lavoro inclusivo, collaborativo e stimolante, valorizzando le persone attraverso percorsi di crescita, formazione, welfare e opportunità di lavoro flessibile.


Abbracciare e celebrare la diversità è fondamentale per il Purpose di HEINEKEN: "Creare il piacere di stare insieme per ispirare un mondo migliore". Riteniamo che le persone, diverse come le nostre birre, anche e non solo per genere, etnia, età, orientamento sessuale, credo religioso, nazionalità, estrazione sociale, disabilità e stile di pensiero, arricchiscano le nostre vite e rafforzino la nostra attività. Accogliamo e prendiamo in considerazione le candidature di tutte le persone qualificate, in quanto sosteniamo una cultura di appartenenza che offre opportunità eque e paritarie per tuttə.



LA POSIZIONE E IL TEAM



HEINEKEN Italia è alla ricerca di un/una Total Rewards Trainee da inserire all’interno della funzione People presso il nostro HQ di Sesto San Giovanni (MI).



SEDE DI LAVORO: Sesto San Giovanni



FUNZIONE: People



ATTIVITA’:


* Analisi dei dati retributivi interni e di mercato (FMCG \& All Market)
* Gestione dei piani di incentivazione variabile (Short term incentive – Sales incentives)
* Analisi dei costi C\&B; e predisposizione stime di impatto sul costo del lavoro (fix and variable pay) e supporto nella definizione del budget annuale
* Stesura delle job descriptions (intervista, skills analysis, preparazione della job e pesatura con il supporto del responsabile C\&B;)
* Supporto nelle definizione/applicazione dei piani di retention e di carriera
* Analisi a supporto dell’Annual Salary Review del Gruppo
* Definizione/revisione delle policy C\&B;
* Partecipazione a progetti ad hoc

REQUISITI:


* Laurea in materie umanistiche/economiche con focus sulla gestione delle Risorse Umane;
* Hai passione per acquisire nuove conoscenze;
* Hai buone capacità di ascolto e ti piace lavorare in team;
* Hai una propensione per i dettagli e attenzione alla pianificazione e alla gestione del tempo, considerato il contesto estremamente dinamico;
* Hai buone capacità analitiche, un forte orientamento al Problem solving e importanti skills di comunicazione
* Hai un’ottima conoscenza del pacchetto Microsoft Office
* Hai una conoscenza fluente della lingua inglese (Listening \& writing);
* Flessibilità, predisposizione al cambiamento, intraprendenza, curiosità e velocità di pensiero ed azione


Perché lavorare con noi?



Entrare in HEINEKEN Italia significa far parte di una realtà dinamica, internazionale e in continua evoluzione, dove ogni persona ha l’opportunità di fare la differenza.  

Crediamo nella libertà di esprimere idee, nel valore della collaborazione e nello sviluppo continuo delle competenze individuali.  

Ci impegniamo ogni giorno per costruire un ambiente inclusivo, sostenibile e appassionante, dove il lavoro ha un impatto reale e ogni giornata è un’occasione per imparare, contribuire e divertirsi.



Segui la stella – scopri HEINEKEN e preparati a brillare ogni giorno, iniziando con energia e terminando con soddisfazione.

title: Corporate Paralegal
description:
Cerchiamo un/una *Corporate Paralegal* con una solida esperienza maturata in studi legali, da inserire nello *staff* del *Corporate Desk* della sede di Milano, con inquadramento al 3° del livello CCNL Studi Professionali.

Di seguito viene riportata una panoramica delle attività richieste.

* Supporto e assistenza ai professionisti di tutti i dipartimenti (*corporate finance*, *real estate*, contenzioso, tax) nei vari adempimenti societari da compiersi presso il Registro delle Imprese, Agenzia delle Entrate e altri uffici competenti, assicurando adeguato monitoraggio e *feedback* (quali, a titolo esemplificativo e non esaustivo, depositi, estrazione certificazioni camerali).
* Preparazione della modulistica e gestione dei processi connessi alle attività da compiersi nei confronti degli uffici pubblici.
* Preparazione della modulistica e gestione dei processi connessi all’ottenimento di firme digitali, Codici Fiscali e PEC.
* Buona conoscenza dei modelli ministeriali dell’Agenzia delle Entrate, per modifiche societarie.
* Tenuta e aggiornamento libri sociali, con verifica della correttezza formale e sostanziale della documentazione.
* Protocollazione, tracciamento e archiviazione ordinata e sistematica della documentazione societaria, assicurandone completezza e reperibilità.
* Supporto operativo ai/alle professionisti/e nelle attività societarie connesse alla conclusione di operazioni straordinarie, con progressiva autonomia nella gestione delle pratiche più complesse.
* Attività di aggiornamento nelle procedure amministrative e pratiche societarie, con contributo al miglioramento e alla standardizzazione dei processi interni.
* Gestione e supervisione di attività eventualmente demandate a fornitori esterni nella finalizzazione di documenti e pratiche societarie (notai, agenzie di servizi, ecc.), fungendo da punto di riferimento operativo.
* Conoscenza di Telemaco e capacità di esaminare estratti camerali e dei pubblici registri e visure, con analisi critica delle informazioni.
* Interfaccia diretta con professionisti/e, clienti e *stakeholder* esterni per il coordinamento delle attività societarie.
* Monitoraggio autonomo delle scadenze e gestione degli adempimenti societari (approvazione bilanci, nomine cariche sociali), garantendo qualità e tempestività degli *output*.
* Supporto qualificato e, ove necessario, gestione autonoma di specifiche fasi di costituzione di società e operazioni straordinarie (fusioni, scissioni, trasformazioni, aumenti di capitale).
* Revisione della documentazione societaria e contributo alla preparazione di verbali societari (CdA, assemblee).
* Possibile affiancamento e supporto operativo a risorse più *junior* del *team*.
* Conoscenza della lingua inglese (scritta e parlata) a livello B1/B2\.
* Buona conoscenza di internet e dei siti camerali.

Una pregressa esperienza nella preparazione di verbali societari verrà considerata come elemento positivo.

Completano il profilo precisione, affidabilità, riservatezza, ottime capacità organizzative e attenzione alle scadenze.

La risorsa avrà l'opportunità di crescere in un ambiente di lavoro stimolante, ricco di talento e in continua espansione.

Legance è uno Studio legale indipendente con uffici a Milano, Roma e Londra. Fondato nel 2007, si distingue oggi nel mercato legale quale autorevole partner di riferimento per clienti e istituzioni.

Lo Studio è sempre cresciuto attraendo giovani talenti ma anche professionisti affermati sul mercato che hanno condiviso il progetto e i principi fondanti. Legance infatti, è anche la condivisione di una grande esperienza professionale dove, oltre all’attenzione per i clienti, si vive un forte spirito di appartenenza da parte di professionisti e dipendenti.

Si prega di scrivere all’indirizzo *email* recruiting@legance.it citando nell’oggetto Paralegal Corporate.

Contratto di lavoro: Tempo pieno, Tempo indeterminato

Retribuzione: €35\.000,00 \- €38\.000,00 all'anno

Benefit:

* Bevande gratis
* Buoni pasto
* Computer aziendale

title: Sales Specialist- Novara
description:
Grazie alle recenti acquisizioni di Juice, Rekordata, Med Computer, Med Innovations e ICT, avvenuta nel 2024, **R\-Store** è oggi il principale partner Apple in Italia e tra i più rilevanti al mondo. Con headquarter a Napoli e una presenza in 18 regioni, l’azienda conta 100 negozi, 900 collaboratori e un fatturato di 600 milioni di euro. Oltre al canale retail consumer, supportato da personale altamente qualificato, R\-Store offre soluzioni su misura per aziende, scuole, università e pubblica amministrazione, garantendo un servizio post\-vendita completo come Centro di Assistenza Autorizzato Apple. Dal 2022 il gruppo ha diversificato il business entrando nei settori della Cyber Security e dei dispositivi ricondizionati, fino ad approdare nel 2024 al mondo della fotografia d'autore con l'apertura del primo Leica Store a Napoli

  

  

**Posizione:**
--------------

In ambito Retail (Vendita, Service, Customer Relation) cerchiamo Apple Sales Specialist per il nostro punto vendita di **Novara**.

 **Cosa farai:**

* Accoglierai ogni cliente con un approccio consulenziale, trasformando ogni interazione in un’esperienza significativa.
* Capirai i bisogni, proporrai soluzioni integrate – prodotti, accessori, protezioni, servizi finanziari e noleggio operativo.
* Cura dell'esposizione dei prodotti nella show room secondo il planogramma schema espositivo) e relativi aggiornamenti, previsti da Apple e da R\-Store.
* Aggiornamento e corretta esposizione del materiale di merchandising.
* Ti relazionerai anche con clienti business e professionisti, offrendo soluzioni su misura grazie alla collaborazione con Rekordata, la società del gruppo dedicata al mondo aziendale
* Ti formerai continuamente grazie alla nostra Academy e ai programmi ufficiali Apple, per diventare un vero esperto della Customer Experience.
* Primo supporto nella risoluzione di problematica di utilizzo dispositivi Apple.

**Requisiti:**
--------------

**Chi cerchiamo?**

  

* Persone curiose con passione per la tecnologia e soprattutto del mondo Apple
* Capacità di ascolto, empatia e orientamento al risultato.
* Esperienza in ambito Retail e preferibilmente nell’elettronica di consumo.
* Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese.

**Altre informazioni:**
-----------------------

**Cosa offriamo:**

  

* Retribuzione fissa (RAL) e bonus mensile fino al 30%, legato a obiettivi chiari misurabili.
* Crescita professionale in un’azienda solida, in espansione e con valori condivisi.

  

* Un sistema di incentivazione meritocratico basato su risultati misurabili.

  

* Percorsi di formazione certificata Apple, con sviluppo di competenze su vendita consulenziale, comunicazione e customer experience.

  

* Opportunità di crescita reale all’interno del gruppo R\-Store, sia nel canale retail che in aree specialistiche come business, education e digital solutions.

  

* Inquadramento: RAL: 23\.000 \- 25\.000 \- 5/ 4 livello CCNL Commercio e Terziario

 **Perché R\-Store**


R\-Store come principale Premium Partner del brand, è a capo di un ecosistema strategico che unisce eccellenze complementari: Rekordata, che trasforma il business con soluzioni tecnologiche su misura, e Clever, il cuore green del gruppo dedicato ai prodotti ricondizionati e alla sostenibilità.


Inoltre, entrerai a far parte di un ambiente in cui le persone rappresentano il valore più importante. Crediamo che il successo di ogni progetto, decisione e innovazione nasca dal contributo di chi lo rende possibile ogni giorno. Per questo mettiamo collaboratori, clienti e partner al centro di ogni processo, valorizzandone competenze, idee e potenziale. In R\-Store promuoviamo una cultura basata sull’ascolto, sulla collaborazione e sulla crescita reciproca, perché siamo convinti che siano le persone a fare davvero la differenza

title: MEDIATORE CREDITIZIO - CREDIT ADVISOR IN SEDE A MILANO
description:

title: Farmacista Collaboratore/Collaboratrice - Milano
description:
Unisciti al nostro team e contribuisci al benessere delle persone!
  
  

Chi siamo
  
  

Farma Acquisition Holding, parte del The Boots Group, è un Retailer Farmaceutico che gestisce oltre 90 Farmacie di proprietà presenti in più di 10 regioni italiane.
  
  

Le nostre radici affondano negli anni '70 con Alleanza Farmaceutica, storica realtà della Distribuzione all'ingrosso di farmaci. Da questa esperienza è nato il nostro percorso nella gestione diretta delle Farmacie, con l'obiettivo di offrire un servizio sempre più vicino alle persone e ai bisogni di salute delle comunità locali.
  
  

Nel 2018 Boots è arrivata in Italia, portando con sé una storia iniziata nel 1849 nel Regno Unito e un'esperienza riconosciuta a livello internazionale nel campo della salute, del benessere, dell'innovazione e della qualità del servizio.
  
  

In Boots crediamo che la Farmacia sia molto più di un luogo dove dispensare farmaci: è uno spazio di ascolto, prevenzione e consulenza, in cui ogni professionista può contribuire concretamente al benessere delle persone e fare la differenza ogni giorno.
  
  

Per le nostre Farmacie di Milano siamo alla ricerca di un/una Farmacista che desideri crescere in un ambiente dinamico, innovativo e orientato alla qualità del servizio.
  
  

Principali attività
  
  

La risorsa sarà inserita all’interno della Farmacia e contribuirà allo svolgimento delle attività professionali, operative e gestionali necessarie al corretto funzionamento del punto vendita.
  
  

Si occuperà della dispensazione di farmaci, parafarmaci e prodotti per la salute, della consulenza qualificata al cliente/paziente e della vendita assistita di dispositivi medici, integratori, prodotti dermocosmetici, articoli sanitari e prodotti per il benessere.
  
  

Parteciperà inoltre all’erogazione dei Servizi di Farmacia e della “Farmacia dei Servizi”, inclusi Servizi di screening, prevenzione, autoanalisi, prenotazioni, telemedicina e altre prestazioni attivabili secondo la normativa vigente.
  
  

La risorsa supporterà il Team nelle attività di gestione del magazzino, riordino, controllo delle scorte, verifica delle scadenze, corretta conservazione dei prodotti, utilizzo dei gestionali aziendali e gestione degli adempimenti amministrativi e documentali.
  
  

Collaborerà, inoltre, al rispetto della normativa di settore e delle procedure relative ai rapporti con ASL/ATS ed enti competenti, alla gestione dei farmaci stupefacenti, alla tracciabilità, alla farmacovigilanza e agli ulteriori adempimenti previsti per l’esercizio dell’attività farmaceutica.
  
  

Completano le attività la collaborazione con il Team di Farmacia, il rispetto delle procedure aziendali e il contributo al mantenimento di elevati standard di qualità del servizio, efficienza gestionale e continuità operativa.
  
  

Competenze e capacità richieste
  
  

Cerchiamo una persona con solida preparazione professionale, orientamento alla qualità del servizio e attenzione alla relazione con il cliente/paziente.
  
  

Sono richieste capacità di ascolto, comunicazione e consulenza, unite a precisione, affidabilità e senso di responsabilità nello svolgimento delle attività quotidiane.
  
  

Completano il profilo spirito di collaborazione, flessibilità organizzativa, attitudine al lavoro in team e interesse a contribuire all’evoluzione della farmacia come presidio sanitario di prossimità, nel rispetto degli standard professionali, aziendali e normativi.
  
  

Cosa offriamo
  
CCNL Farmacie Private
  
Contratto a tempo indeterminato (o tempo determinato per i neolaureati)
  
Formazione continua in ambito scientifico, consulenziale e gestionale
  
Sistema incentivante legato ai risultati
  
Percorsi di crescita professionale all'interno del Gruppo
  
Ambiente collaborativo e orientato al benessere delle persone
  
Work\-life balance garantito
  
  

Retribuzione e benefit
  
RAL indicativa tra € 30\.000 ed € 36\.000, commisurata all'esperienza e alle competenze
  
Buoni pasto
  
Sistema premiante annuale
  
Gettone domeniche e festivi
  
Percorsi di sviluppo professionale e manageriale
  
  

Fai la differenza nella vita delle persone unendo la tua professionalità alla nostra missione!
  
Se sei un/una Farmacista appassionato/a, motivato/a e desideroso/a di lavorare in un contesto dinamico e innovativo, non esitare a candidarti.
  
  

Farma Acquisition Holding \- Farmacie Boots ti aspetta per costruire insieme un futuro dedicato alla salute e al benessere.

title: TECNICO CERTIFICAZIONE DI PRODOTTO JUNIOR
Director, Facilities Design & Utilities
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**

**Job Title:** Director, Facilities Design \& Utilities  

**Reports To:** Jaime Romo, Head of Global Engineering Services, Pharma Services Group

**Position Summary**


The Director, Facilities Design \& Utilities is a senior engineering leader responsible for the strategic planning, design, and execution support of facility infrastructure across the Pharma Services Group. This role ensures that all facility systems—including architectural fit and finish, electrical distribution, water systems, and HVAC—are designed, implemented, and maintained to meet regulatory, operational, and business requirements. This individual is the Business Process Owner for Facilities Design \& Utilities for PSG.


The incumbent will drive standardization, operational excellence, and innovation across global sites, enabling reliable, compliant, and scalable manufacturing environments that support current and future business needs.

**Key Responsibilities**

* **Strategic Leadership**
	+ Define and implement the global strategy for facility design and utility systems across Pharma Services.
	+ Partner with Engineering, Operations, Quality, and EHS to align infrastructure capabilities with business growth and regulatory expectations.
	+ Serve as a technical authority and advisor to senior leadership on facility and utility\-related investments and risks.
* **Facility Design \& Execution**
	+ Oversee end\-to\-end facility design, including architectural layout, fit and finish, and space optimization for GMP environments.
	+ Ensure designs meet applicable regulatory standards (FDA, EMA, ISO) and internal quality requirements.
	+ Lead capital project design reviews and provide governance to ensure consistency, cost\-effectiveness, and scalability.
* **Utilities \& Infrastructure Oversight**
	+ Own engineering standards and performance for critical utility systems, including:
		- Electrical distribution and backup systems
		- Water systems (e.g., WFI, purified water, wastewater)
		- HVAC systems (including cleanroom environments and environmental controls)
	+ Establish global standards for reliability, redundancy, and energy efficiency.
* **Operational Excellence**
	+ Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance system performance, reduce downtime, and optimize lifecycle costs.
	+ Implement standardized design practices, specifications, and playbooks across sites.
	+ Leverage digital tools and data analytics to monitor and improve facility and utility performance.
* **Compliance \& Risk Management**
	+ Ensure all facility and utility systems comply with cGMP, safety, and environmental regulations.
	+ Support inspections and audits, addressing facility\-related observations and driving corrective actions.
	+ Identify and mitigate risks related to infrastructure reliability and capacity.
* **Stakeholder \& Team Leadership**
	+ Lead and develop a high\-performing team of engineers and technical experts.
	+ Collaborate with global site leaders and project teams to ensure successful project delivery.
	+ Manage external partners, including engineering firms, contractors, and equipment vendors.

**Minimum Requirements**

* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, Chemical, or related field); advanced degree preferred.
* 12\+ years of progressive experience in facility engineering, design, and utilities within regulated industries (pharmaceutical, biotech, or related).
* Demonstrated leadership experience managing global or multi\-site teams and large\-scale capital projects.
* Deep technical expertise in facility systems, including HVAC, electrical, and water systems in GMP environments.
* Strong knowledge of regulatory requirements (FDA, EMA, cGMP).
* International Travel 25%

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Professional Engineer (PE) license or equivalent certification.
* Experience leading global standardization initiatives and engineering governance models.
* Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders and drive cross\-functional alignment.
* Experience leading a team of design engineers
* Experience in FDA or similar regulatory audits.
* Strong financial acumen, including capital planning and cost management.

**Leadership Competencies**

* Sets strategic direction and translates vision into execution across multiple sites.
* Drives innovation and continuous improvement at scale.
* Builds and develops high\-performing, diverse teams.
* Influences across functions and geographies to achieve enterprise outcomes.
* Demonstrates strong business acumen and accountability for results.
* May lead a team of 1 to 5 engineers.

**Impact**


This role is critical to ensuring that Pharma Services facilities are designed and operated to the highest standards of quality, compliance, and efficiency—directly enabling Thermo Fisher Scientific’s mission to enable customers to make the world healthier, cleaner, and safer.

title: PPI & Digital Project Manager
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**

**DESCRIPTION**


Join the PPI Digital Transformation organization at Thermo Fisher Scientific as a **Digital Project Manager**, where you will be responsible for the end\-to\-end delivery of digital initiatives across manufacturing sites.


In this role, you will translate business and operational needs into executable digital projects, coordinating cross\-functional teams that include Data Engineering, Data Science, IIoT, IT, Automation, and site stakeholders.


You will own execution, planning, risk management, and change management for assigned initiatives, ensuring that solutions are delivered safely, compliantly, and with verified business impact.


The position operates in a **multi\-site, matrix environment** and works in close collaboration with technical domain leads and senior technical experts, while reporting directly to the PPI Digital Transformation leadership.


Strong stakeholder management, execution discipline, and focus on value realization are critical for success.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Digital Project Delivery**

* Manage the full lifecycle of assigned digital initiatives, from opportunity framing through deployment and value realization.
* Translate business needs into clear project charters, scope, milestones, resource plans, and success criteria, using PPI and Lean principles.
* Build and maintain detailed project plans, RAID logs, and status reporting; proactively identify risks and define mitigation actions.
* Coordinate cross\-functional teams and external partners to ensure on\-time, on\-budget, and in\-scope delivery.

**Digital Roadmap \& Portfolio Support**

* Support the prioritization and sequencing of digital use cases in alignment with site strategy, PPI targets, and divisional roadmaps.
* Provide visibility on capacity, dependencies, and constraints across parallel initiatives to enable realistic planning.
* Contribute to annual and multi\-year digital planning activities.

**Business Case \& Benefits Realization**

* Develop robust business cases for digital initiatives in collaboration with site functions.
* Track benefits using a bottom\-up approach aligned with site PPI savings plans.
* Define and monitor KPIs for each initiative; ensure benefits are validated with Finance and business owners.
* Provide accurate input to digital and PPI performance reviews and dashboards.

**Stakeholder Engagement \& Change Management**

* Act as the primary point of contact for assigned digital initiatives at site level.
* Lead change management activities, including stakeholder mapping, communication plans, training coordination, and adoption tracking.
* Facilitate workshops, Kaizens, and design sessions to define current state, future state, and implementation roadmaps.
* Ensure digital solutions are embedded into standard work and PPI routines, avoiding isolated pilots without sustained adoption.

**Governance, Compliance \& Standards**

* Apply divisional PPI \& Digital governance models, standards, and templates consistently.
* Ensure alignment with IT/OT architecture, cybersecurity, data standards, and validation requirements.
* Coordinate with IT, OT, and Data \& Analytics teams to ensure data quality and compliance with GxP, data integrity, and privacy requirements.

**Site \& Collaboration Model**


The role supports digital initiatives across multiple manufacturing sites, operating in a **distributed, cross\-site model** and collaborating with stakeholders across EU and APAC locations. Occasional travel to sites may be required.

**REQUIREMENTS**

**Minimum Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Life Sciences, Business, or related field.
* **5\+ years of experience** in project management, continuous improvement, operations, or digital transformation roles within manufacturing or regulated environments.
* Proven experience delivering digital, automation, data/analytics, or manufacturing IT initiatives.
* Strong project management skills, including scope definition, planning, stakeholder management, and risk control.
* Solid understanding of GMP manufacturing and quality processes.
* Fluency in English and Italian.

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Master’s degree in a relevant field.
* Experience in pharmaceutical, biotech, CDMO, or other highly regulated industries.
* Familiarity with MES, LIMS, historians, data platforms, and BI tools.
* Experience with Agile or hybrid delivery approaches.
* Lean / Six Sigma / PPI certification (Green Belt or equivalent).
* Formal project management certification (PMP, PRINCE2\).
* Change management certification (e.g. PROSCI).

**Key Competencies \& Soft Skills**

* Strong stakeholder management and communication skills across all organizational levels.
* Ability to work effectively in a **matrix and cross\-functional environment**.
* Structured, analytical, and results\-oriented mindset.
* Ability to balance execution discipline with flexibility and “test \& learn” approaches.
* High learning agility and curiosity toward digital technologies and new ways of working.
* Strong commitment to safety, quality, compliance, and company values.

**Additional Information**

* Travel may be required (**up to 30%**).

title: Quality Project Compliance Associate
description:
**Attività principali:**

* Partecipare attivamente come funzione Qualità alla definizione e alla progettazione di nuovi processi produttivi, a partire dalle fasi di sviluppo fino al trasferimento in GMP, in collaborazione con le unità Development e MSAT.
* Coordinare tutti gli aspetti di qualità e regolatori associati all'introduzione di nuovi progetti, effettuando una puntuale valutazione dei rischi connessi.
* Rivedere e approvare la documentazione tecnica relativa al trasferimento di nuovi processi.
* Coordinare la prima emissione del Product Specification File (PSF) per i processi che entrano in GMP a seguito di lotti di sviluppo o trasferimento tecnologico (Tech Transfer).
* Partecipare come membro attivo ai core team di progetto, sia interni che esterni.
* Fungere da referente diretto del sito per la controparte Qualità dei clienti per tutte le attività di competenza.
* Partecipare in qualità SME a ispezioni da parte di clienti, enti corporate e autorità regolatorie per le aree di propria competenza.

**Requisiti:**

* Laurea scientifica (es. Biotecnologie, CTF)
* esperienza in Qualità farmaceutica di almeno due anni
* Inglese fluente
* conoscenze di Introduzione Nuovi Processi (NPI) sarà considerata un plus
* buone capacità relazionali e di negoziazione


Salary range: 34\.000€\-40\.000€

**Who We Are**  

*AGC Biologics is a global Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization with a strong presence in Italy. From our Milan site, we develop and manufacture life‑changing advanced biologics for some of the world’s most innovative pharmaceutical companies. Together with our team members across Europe, the U.S., and Japan, we deliver development and manufacturing of mammalian and microbial\-based therapeutic proteins, plasmid DNA (pDNA), messenger RNA (mRNA), viral vectors, and genetically engineered cells therapies. Across our global network, we are united by one mission: To work side by side with our customers in order to improve patients’ lives by bringing new biopharmaceuticals to market.*
